title: My Rock Bottom
content: Twenty-six years old. Maxed credit cards. Negative bank account. Eating ramen not by choice. I remember sitting on my apartment floor thinking: 'How did I get here?' That's when I started learning. Really learning.
extended_content: I wasn't stupid. I had a college degree. I had a job. But I had zero systems. Every paycheck disappeared without me knowing where it went. The wake-up call wasn't the overdraft fees - it was the shame. Not being able to go out with friends. Making excuses. Pretending everything was fine. That moment on the floor was when I decided: never again.
when_to_tell: when someone shares their own money struggles
lesson: Rock bottom can be the beginning, not the end
